title: ROSÉ and Bruno Mars Perform "APT." at 2026 Grammys
text: ROSÉ, a member of BLACKPINK, made history at the 2026 Grammy Awards by becoming "the first solo K-pop artist to perform on Music's Biggest Night." She performed her global hit "APT." alongside collaborator Bruno Mars, who played electric guitar during the energetic stage show. The performance featured Mars delivering a guitar solo while smoke enveloped the stage as ROSÉ moved across the platform with infectious energy. The collaboration achieved massive international success, topping charts in more than 50 countries. The track earned multiple Grammy nominations, making ROSÉ the inaugural K-pop artist nominated as a primary performer for one of the four major award categories. "APT." received nominations for Record of the Year, Song of the Year, and Best Pop Duo/Group Performance. "APT." served as the lead single from ROSÉ's 2024 debut solo album rosie. Meanwhile, BLACKPINK is preparing to release a new mini album titled [DEADLINE] on February 27th, following their reunion for a world tour the previous year. Bruno Mars is currently preparing his album The Romantic, also arriving February 27th, with a supporting stadium tour in the works.
